reviews.author: Violet Bethany Ball
reviews.text: We spent the morning here and loved it. We got there at around 930 and almost had it to ourselves. It was beautifully maintained and really clear clean water. No rubbish anywhere. This lovely nature escape was such a nice find for us. You can walk around through the rainforest bamboo pathways for 80k dong but we paid the full 190k dong to give access to the two swimming areas. It was well worth the money. This place is really big and beautiful. They do make you wear a life jacket to swim, we are both strong swimmers and wish we didn't have to, but I do understand as the currents are strong and they have life guards everywhere to keep people safe and the jackets would help with visibility. So we understood this requirement keeping everyone safe who might not be able to swim against currents well.

reviews.author: Lynn Ho
reviews.text: I have to thank Nam, the driver from Phong Nha Rice Field Homestay. Who took us here to see the beauty of Mooc Spring. We really loved to walk on the bamboo bridges along the river, to see the nature deep down the waterfalls. All you hear is water running through the rocks, the birds singing and the sounds of vietnam Jungle. This is so stunning to be here with mother nature of Mooc Spring. We sat there, at a resting place in the middle of junhle, it was simply built with all the bamboo, surrounding by water, trees, mosses, lichens, ferns, mushrooms...birds...butterflies...We ❤️ it, the sound of jungle.

reviews.author: Stuart Burckhardt
reviews.text: Certainly beautiful and kinda fun. However, we went here before going to the Dark Caves which has better water activities. We paid 180,000 per person to enter, swim and kayak. We were the ONLY people in the park on that dark which was kinda cool. However, the Kayak area is small, there is a high jump (4m) into the water which is ok. However, if you only have one day I would recommend Dark Caves instead which is much bigger and far better water activities.

reviews.author: Cara Neumann
reviews.text: This area is so beautiful to walk around and chill out, with great photo opportunities. Where it lacks is I was underwhelmed by the activities. Literally one very small area in which to kayak and swim( the same common photo you see on here). We walked over the bamboo bridges and found a beautiful spot with a slide and ladders down to the water but we're told we couldn't swim there. So honestly the extra money you pay is not really worth it. Staff were few and far between and not very helpful. Very little signage around to help guide you through the area. I understand the natural beauty and don't expect it to be ruined with signage but then maybe when you buy your ticket the staff could inform you of how it all works. You are just left to figure it out yourself. Slightly underwhelmed but the area is stunning!

reviews.author: Daen Jiell
reviews.text: lots of boats & kayaks in a secluded section of the stream. looks more like an amusement facility and doesn‘t give natural vibes bc of all the boats and jumping points etc. However if you want a quick splash on a hot day it‘s still a good option

reviews.author: Huyen Tran
reviews.text: Price ticket for visitting only is 80k person, for full package (including swimming games) is 180k person. Highly recommended to go here in the sunny hot day, best time is around late March to June (June-August probably a bit crowded due to students has summer vacation). We went here in September, in the rainy day. The water is too cold to swim, and the stream is a bit violent as well. The walking is only about 30 minutes. Overall only go there in the sunny day. Rainy day would not recommend.

reviews.author: Tiago Pereira
reviews.text: Beautiful Setting. Not worth the price. 80k to walk around (15/20 mins) 180k to swim and access to kayaks, Rubber boats, etc

reviews.author: Quốc Vương Hà
reviews.text: It's about over 60 km from center of Dong Hoi and only 5 km from Paradise Cave. I recommend you go to Mooc Spring to have lunch after visiting Paradise Cave. There is a large restaurant here that have some dishes and famous by grilled-chicken eaten with sticky rice. The restaurant is a large wooden tent have many clean corner for eating and taking a nap. Grilled-chicken is good (but not good as at East Hill). The tourists have to buy spring entrance ticket. The landscape is attractive. There is a long bamboo-bridge from the gate to the main part of the spring. The spring is very clean, cool and quite deep (available life jacket for swimming). The mountain, the trees and the spring are all dreamy, that impress you so much. Mooc spring is a must visit place to take pictures and enjoy the cuisine.

reviews.author: Vincent G
reviews.text: A funny and nice place to cool down, i think but i am not sure it was 120.000 dong P.P. We as couple where the only tourist there and it was very funny to see the locals swimming with their life jackets on. +you can jump from a bridge( not that high) in the water. + nice view +cool water - the kayaking is not that special

reviews.author: Hoang Nguyen
reviews.text: Easy to access an amazing landscape of the spring and the wood. You can do kayaking, swimming and having local foods. A plus for eco-friendly design with bamboo bridges, bamboo trashes. Wc, bathroom, changing room need to be innovated.

reviews.author: Mason Phung
reviews.text: a nice place to swim, better to come at noon or at least after 10 am traditional vietnamese food, they offer you plenty of options: chicken, pork, shrimp and fish

reviews.author: Brandon
reviews.text: Don’t come here. There are 2 options, one to just walk around the place and another to pay to do the water activities. We chose to just walk around and a male staff member followed us the entire way around, I guess to make sure we didn’t try any activities. It was really unsettling and made us very uncomfortable so we couldn’t enjoy the experience.

reviews.author: mark thomas
reviews.text: Because of storm surge in water levels no water activities available on this day. But the 80,000 VND for the nature walk was well worth it. If you have the time and it is so close to both Paradise Cave and Dark Cave I would recommend you take this walk through nature.

reviews.author: Lee Anh
reviews.text: Beautiful area that really natural. There is a nice stream in the green forest. You can swim and go camping with friends/family. They serve fired-blackcock dishes that really delicious. Be careful with slippery stones under the stream when you swim there

reviews.author: Jennifer Pham
reviews.text: Thế water is so cold but i really happy to spend time there . You should wear life vest even you know how to swimming

reviews.author: Michal Mendelson
reviews.text: Not recommended! We came with the kids, and they were afraid to swim alongside small crabs and couldn’t manage the kayak. The place is small. There’s no proper storage except for small lockers without a key, and the changing rooms are terrible. We asked to leave after a few minutes, but they refused to give us a refund, with some unclear excuse that it’s a non-refundable ticket. Really embarrassing service.

reviews.author: Anita Laffey
reviews.text: We went to Mooc Springs expecting water activities however the lady at the desk told us the currents were too strong for any water activities which we understood. We paid 80k dong to go for a short walk (800m) around the area but were followed the entire time by one of the workers there. It was impossible to enjoy with him following us 3 metres behind at all times. He would stop when we stopped and walk when we walked and made for a very uncomfortable and creepy experience. Would not recommend coming here.

reviews.author: Tanguy Bibus
reviews.text: Very good because we went on off season. It seems to have way to many people in the summer. It is a very good value for money in phong nha.

reviews.author: Inge Hansum
reviews.text: I had an amazing time here, walking on the beautifull bamboo bridges and enjoying the blue water. It is very serene and beautifull here

reviews.author: Minh Tam Duy
reviews.text: Nice place, water is very clean and cool. Should go there before 10AM to enjoy the silence.

reviews.author: Linh Hoang
reviews.text: You can buy 2 type of tickets here: 1 for sightseeing only 2 for swimming, sightseeing and kayak

reviews.author: Daniel Trommer
reviews.text: Mooc Spring offers a refreshing experience with its clear and inviting waters. However, the small size of the pools might feel somewhat restrictive. A notable drawback for European visitors is the mandatory use of buoyant vests while swimming, which can feel unusual and detract from the overall enjoyment. Additionally, the prices are on the higher side.

reviews.author: Bảo Yến Nguyễn Thị
reviews.text: Blue water. Inside have 2 zone. Zone 1 the ticket is 80,000dong/ person. Zone 2 is about 150,000 dong/ person

reviews.author: Marianne Brooks
reviews.text: This is a bad review, and I’ll preface by saying we went on their off season so we weren’t really here to explore their activities, just to do the walking trails and enjoy the view before heading to the dark caves. That being said, the woman at the desk explained that there were two walking trails available. The first didn’t have a ladder down and was inaccessible so we were rerouted to the second which was under construction. The staff told us we could continue on that path despite the construction but there were 10 men hauling bamboo and building the walkway, at which point I just said it wasn’t worth it. I tried explaining to the front desk person that neither trail was available and that it just felt they lacked transparency, but she said since they don’t do refunds she can only give me half of our money back. I felt it was a bit of a scam and worth warning people who pass by the attraction.

reviews.author: Hà Trương
reviews.text: Beautiful restaurant, delicious foods, the prices are quite cheap. Good choice for your family.

reviews.author: Oanh Nguyen
reviews.text: Clear and clean, super cool water. Really enjoyed swimming in it. Very crowded

reviews.author: Ari Good
reviews.text: The swimming is terrible you are made to wear a life jacket and there are so many people. They should not have even sold us a ticket to swim if it was full. They had no kayaks only small rafts with broken in half kayak paddles. The swimming was in the same spot as the kayaking which made it impossible to move. Overall, a complete scam and the worst money I have spent in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Magda B
reviews.text: Not worth it. Better go to dark cave complex, much more water fun (slides, ziplines, sup, etc). Here there is only very small area to swim plus you still need to be in life vest all the time, idiotic. All restaurant parts seem abandoned and services area is beaten down

reviews.author: Duy Nguyễn Văn
reviews.text: Very good to go there, cool water with several activities. But it might be crowded during holiday and weekend.

reviews.author: Niccolò Vitali
reviews.text: Touristic, no authenticity, you can have fun but I came to Vietnam for nature and wildlife, not for doing waterslides and riding inflatables
